text.title: Trello Kanban Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying trello-kanban. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent burns 50% of rate-limit budget on list/label name lookups; 429s hit during bulk writes.
antipattern.root-cause: Each card write fetches lists/labels by name and resolves the ID inline.
antipattern.fix: Cache board/list/label IDs once per session in memory or constitution.md. Subsequent writes use cached IDs only.

antipattern.symptom: Cards moved to Done via API are never archived; the Done list grows; rollups break.
antipattern.root-cause: Butler rules trigger on UI events only. API-driven moves do not fire Butler automations.
antipattern.fix: After moving to Done via API, explicitly call PUT /cards/{id} with closed=true. Document this in constitution.md.

antipattern.symptom: 20+ labels; filters return wrong subsets; team gives up on labels.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents invent new labels per card instead of using the closed taxonomy.
antipattern.fix: Enforce <=12 labels in the type/priority/modifier taxonomy. Reject labels created outside the taxonomy in PR review.

antipattern.symptom: "[5pt] Add OAuth" titles; story points cannot be queried via API; rollups broken.
antipattern.root-cause: Custom Fields Power-Up not enabled; data encoded in titles.
antipattern.fix: Enable Custom Fields Power-Up. Migrate title-embedded data to fields. Update agents to write fields, not parse titles.
